summary: Designed comprehensive migration/dedupe plan for Content Library. Identified 9 URL duplicates (8 Calendly, 1 Notion). Proposed safe migration strategy with rollback capability, ID redirect mapping, subtype retrofit, and scrape cleanup.

dedupe_strategy.overview: Three-tier dedupe approach with increasing risk tolerance. Execute in order, verify after each tier before proceeding.

tier_1_url_duplicates.signal_strength: STRONG (URL is canonical identifier for links)
tier_1_url_duplicates.resolution_strategy: Keep calendly_* IDs as canonical (deterministic, tied to active Calendly sync). Merge curated tags from legacy IDs, then deprecate legacy IDs.
tier_1_url_duplicates.risk: LOW - URL uniqueness is a strong signal; only risk is losing curated tags if not merged

tier_2_title_normalization.approach: Normalize titles (lowercase, strip punctuation, collapse whitespace) and flag items with same (content_type, normalized_title) for manual review
candidates: vrijen_* scheduling IDs that duplicate calendly_* after URL dedup is done
Items with path-style titles like 'meeting_booking / vrijen_only / work_30m_primary'
tier_2_title_normalization.resolution_strategy: Generate review queue; require human confirmation before merge
tier_2_title_normalization.risk: MEDIUM - automated merge could conflate semantically different items

tier_3_external_id_collisions.signal_strength: STRONG (if external_id matches, same external resource)
tier_3_external_id_collisions.current_state: Calendly sync stores UUID in file frontmatter but DB lacks external_id column
tier_3_external_id_collisions.recommendation: Add external_id column to schema; backfill from Calendly UUIDs in file frontmatter
tier_3_external_id_collisions.future_use: Prevents duplicates on re-ingest from any external source

calendly_id_migration_plan.target_canonical_ids: calendly_<slug> format from W1.4 (e.g., calendly_30min, calendly_friends_family_link_for_vrijen)

curated_tags_to_preserve.source: Legacy json_migration records have curated tags (audience, purpose, context, entity, duration)
curated_tags_to_preserve.merge_rule: Copy non-empty curated tags from legacy record to canonical record ONLY if canonical record lacks that tag

redirect_handling.approach: Create redirect table (old_id -> new_id) in DB for API callers
redirect_handling.table_schema: CREATE TABLE id_redirects (old_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, new_id TEXT NOT NULL, migrated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')))
redirect_handling.behavior: Any lookup by old_id should transparently return new_id item
redirect_handling.retention: Keep redirects for 90 days, then archive to JSON log

non_duplicate_legacy_items.note: 5 vrijen_* IDs without Calendly sync counterpart (likely deleted/renamed on Calendly side)
items: vrijen_work_30m_primary (no URL in DB - orphan reference)
vrijen_work_30m_extended (no URL in DB - orphan reference)
vrijen_work_45m (no URL in DB - orphan reference)
vrijen_quick_sync_15m (no URL in DB - orphan reference)
vrijen_friends_family_45m (no URL in DB - orphan reference)
non_duplicate_legacy_items.resolution: Mark deprecated=1, move to archive query. Do NOT delete - may contain curated context

deprecated: list of orphan IDs marked deprecated

subtype_retrofit_plan.principle: Subtypes add finer classification within existing content_types. Do NOT expand top-level types.

link.criteria: url is linkedin.com/in/* OR github.com/* profile page
url points to doc, deck, form (docsend, tally, typeform, notion doc)

article.criteria: author=vrijen OR source_url contains V's domains

implementation.schema_change: ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN subtype TEXT
implementation.backfill_query: UPDATE items SET subtype='scheduling-link' WHERE content_type='link' AND url LIKE '%calendly.com%'

identified_issues.problem: Contains base64-encoded image data (~500KB), presentation UI chrome
identified_issues.action: Extract clean text content, move image to separate asset, add summary in frontmatter
Populate with actual profile content or delete

cleanup_steps: 1. Identify files with >50KB or containing base64 data
2. For each: extract clean text body, strip HTML/base64
3. Generate short summary (manual or LLM) for frontmatter
5. Update DB record with new word_count, has_content flags

migration_steps.name: Add schema columns
migration_steps.action: ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN subtype TEXT; ALTER TABLE items ADD COLUMN external_id TEXT; CREATE TABLE id_redirects (...)
migration_steps.rollback: DROP COLUMN subtype; DROP COLUMN external_id; DROP TABLE id_redirects

migration_steps.name: Merge curated tags
migration_steps.action: For each (old_id, new_id) pair: copy curated tags from old to new if new lacks them
migration_steps.validation: Query tags table to confirm merge

migration_steps.name: Create redirects
migration_steps.action: INSERT INTO id_redirects (old_id, new_id) for each mapping
migration_steps.validation: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM id_redirects should equal mapping count

migration_steps.name: Deprecate legacy duplicates
migration_steps.action: UPDATE items SET deprecated=1 WHERE id IN (old_ids list)
migration_steps.validation: Canonical IDs remain accessible; old_ids return via redirect

migration_steps.name: Deprecate orphans
migration_steps.action: UPDATE items SET deprecated=1 WHERE id IN (orphan vrijen_* list)
migration_steps.validation: These items no longer appear in active queries

migration_steps.name: Backfill subtypes
migration_steps.action: Run subtype UPDATE queries for scheduling-link, profile-link, etc.
migration_steps.validation: SELECT subtype, COUNT(*) FROM items GROUP BY subtype

migration_steps.name: Export mapping artifact
migration_steps.validation: File exists and is valid JSON

risk_notes.risk: Callers using legacy IDs will break
risk_notes.mitigation: id_redirects table provides transparent redirect. Document migration in README.
Merge logic only copies if destination is empty. Pre-migration backup allows recovery.

risk_notes.risk: Orphan vrijen_* items may have unreplaced context
risk_notes.mitigation: Mark deprecated, don't delete. Can be restored if needed.
Conservative rules (only URL-based). Manual review queue for edge cases.

notes_for_orchestrator: SCHEMA ADDITION NEEDED: migration requires adding `subtype` and `external_id` columns to items table, plus `id_redirects` table. Coordinate with W1.2 if schema work is shared.
IMPLEMENTATION SCRIPT: Recommend creating N5/scripts/migrate_content_library_v5.py to execute all migration steps atomically with rollback capability.
CURATED TAG INSIGHT: Legacy json_migration items have useful category_path tags (e.g., 'meeting_booking', 'vrijen_only') that could inform subtype inference. Consider preserving these as metadata.
NON-CALENDLY DUPLICATES: Found 2 non-Calendly URL duplicates (McKinsey Notion doc, Careerspan trial code). These need manual review to determine which is canonical.
ORPHAN COUNT: 5 vrijen_* scheduling IDs exist with no URL and no Calendly counterpart. Likely old manual entries. Safe to deprecate but not delete.
SCRAPE QUALITY: gamma.app file is 500KB+ due to base64 images. Cleanup script should handle this before declaring migration complete.
